titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Charles Albertus Photograph Collection Documenting Life at Yale
Albertus (Charles) Photograph Collection Documenting Life at Yale
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 27, 2018 at 08:56
finding aid revisiion description not supplied

did.unittitle: Charles Albertus photograph collection documenting life at Yale
did.abstract: The materials consist of photographs and negatives taken by Charles Albertus, primarily of Yale buildings, grounds, and individuals. Includes a few photographs of New Haven, Connecticut.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 48-A (YRG 48-A), Yale pictorial records and collections.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Charles Albertus Photograph Collection Documenting Life at Yale (RU 775). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of photographs and negatives taken by Charles Albertus, primarily of Yale buildings, grounds, and individuals. Includes a few photographs of New Haven, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: 5:16 Morse College and 5:17 Ezra Stiles College
5:16 Morse College and 5:17 Ezra Stiles College, Construction negatives
13:18 Becton Engineering and Applied Science Center
20:6 Peabody Museum of Natural History, Interior
20:23 Kline Biology Tower: Roof Topping Party
Carr, Rev. Aute L (at the Divinity School): negatives.
Ripley, S. Dillon: Peabody Museum party
New Haven: Insurance Building, Whitney Ave.
Faculty and staff events: Southeast Asia Studies, Bedoyo dancers
22:167 School of Public Health and Epidemiology
Canfield, Curtis F., MacLeish, Archibald: Production of AJ.B.@
Goetze, Albrecht and Stephens, Ferris J.
Truman, Harry S. and Acheson, Dean Gooderham
Esquire article, 1967: Roberts, Steven V., "Is It Too Late for a Man of Honesty, High Purpose and Intelligence to Be Elected President of the United States in 1968?"
